title: Today's AI Brief: Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build
summary: Microsoft is set to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build, while Anthropic has officially filed to go public.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build
imageCaption: Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've got my coffee in hand, and I'm ready to walk you through the latest headlines in the world of artificial intelligence. It's been a busy week in AI land, and I'm excited to share some of the highlights with you.

First up, Microsoft is gearing up to unveil some exciting new AI models and Windows improvements at their Build conference. I've been following this story on The Verge, and it seems like they're set to introduce a Copilot super app, a new reasoning AI model, and a slew of Windows improvements. The Copilot super app is particularly interesting - it's like a Swiss Army knife for AI-powered productivity. I'm curious to see how this will change the game for Windows users.

In other news, Anthropic has officially filed to go public. This is a big move for the AI startup, and it's clear that they're looking to make a splash in the market. The race between Anthropic and OpenAI is heating up, and I'm curious to see how this plays out. It's always exciting to see new players enter the market and push the boundaries of what's possible with AI.

Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Report AI Tech Microsoft to unveil new AI models and Windows improvements at Build Build will include a Copilot super app, a new reasoning AI model, and lots of Windows improvements. Build will include a Copilot super app, a new reasoning AI model, and lots of Windows improvements. by Tom Warren Jun 1, 2026, 2:39 PM UTC Image: Microsoft Part Of LIVE Microso... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Learn how Endava uses Codex to build an agentic organization, accelerating software delivery and reducing requirements analysis from weeks to hours. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
